notes.text:
MOUNTAIN VALLEY PM&R ASSOCIATES
FACE-TO-FACE EVALUATION FOR POWER MOBILITY DEVICE

Patient: Eleanor M. Vance | DOB: 1954-10-05 | Age: 71 | Sex: F | MRN: EHR-EV-001
Date of Service: 2026-04-15 | Provider: Robert Klein, MD — Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation
NPI: 3456789012
Visit Type: New patient PM&R evaluation; CMS-required face-to-face for power mobility device (PMD)
Time Spent: 60 minutes (>50% counseling and care coordination)

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Progressive lower-extremity weakness and inability to self-propel her current manual wheelchair. Member needs evaluation for an upgraded mobility device to safely perform mobility-related activities of daily living (MRADLs) within her home.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Eleanor Vance is a 71-year-old widowed female with a 17-year history of relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis (now in secondary-progressive phase, diagnosed 2009) who presents for a CMS face-to-face evaluation regarding power mobility device candidacy. She has been a manual wheelchair user since 2018 (Medicare-funded K0001 ultralight). Over the past 12-18 months she reports a progressive functional decline:

- Increasing fatigue with self-propulsion; unable to sustain wheel propulsion for more than 15-20 feet without rest.
- Worsening upper-extremity weakness (R > L); 4-/5 grip strength bilaterally.
- Documented falls: two falls in the past 6 months — one with minor right wrist contusion (urgent care visit 02/03/2026), one without injury (03/12/2026).
- Inability to safely transfer from wheelchair to toilet without assistance; daughter visits 3x/week to assist.
- Bathing: requires shower-chair plus daughter's standby assistance.
- Cannot independently access the kitchen to prepare meals — relies on Meals on Wheels 5 days/week.

She lives alone in a single-family home in Asheville. Her daughter Margaret visits Mon/Wed/Fri to help with bathing, laundry, and meal prep. Without an adequate mobility device, the patient is at increasing risk of falls and is unable to perform routine MRADLs (bathing, dressing, toileting, meal preparation, mobility within the home) safely or independently.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
- Multiple sclerosis (secondary progressive, dx 2009; baseline EDSS 7.0)
- Hypertension, well-controlled on amlodipine 5 mg daily
- Osteopenia (T-score -2.1 lumbar spine; supplemented with vitamin D + calcium)
- Mild cognitive impairment (MoCA 26/30 — adequate cognition for safe PMD operation)
- No history of dementia, seizure disorder, or visual impairment that would preclude safe PMD use

MEDICATIONS
- Ocrelizumab 600 mg IV q6 months (last dose 02/2026)
- Baclofen 10 mg TID (spasticity)
- Amlodipine 5 mg daily
- Vitamin D3 2000 IU + calcium carbonate 600 mg daily
- Acetaminophen 650 mg PRN

ALLERGIES — NKDA

SOCIAL HISTORY — Widowed; lives alone in single-family home with one external step (ramp present, threshold 2 inches). Daughter (Margaret) is primary caregiver. Non-smoker. No alcohol. Retired schoolteacher.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

Vitals: BP 138/82, HR 78, BMI 24.1
General: Alert, oriented x3. Pleasant, no distress at rest.
MSK / Neuro:
- Upper extremity strength: deltoid 4/5 b/l, biceps 4/5 b/l, triceps 4-/5 b/l, grip 4-/5 b/l (R slightly weaker than L).
- Lower extremity strength: hip flexion 2/5 b/l, knee extension 2+/5 b/l, ankle dorsiflexion 1/5 b/l. Significant proximal and distal lower-extremity weakness.
- Tone: increased lower-extremity tone, modified Ashworth 2 b/l hip adductors, knee flexors.
- Sensation: decreased pinprick and proprioception in feet bilaterally; intact above the knees.
- Reflexes: 3+ patellar and Achilles b/l; up-going plantars consistent with corticospinal involvement.
- Coordination: dysmetria on finger-nose-finger b/l (R > L).
- Cranial nerves: II-XII intact except bilateral mild internuclear ophthalmoplegia (chronic).

Functional Mobility (in clinic):
- Sit-to-stand: unable to perform without assistance.
- Standing balance: unable to maintain unsupported standing.
- Manual wheelchair self-propulsion: able to propel 8 feet on level tile before requiring rest; reports significant exertion.
- Transfers: requires moderate assistance of one for wheelchair-to-mat-table.

---

MAE ALGORITHM (Mobility Assistive Equipment) — Step-by-step justification per CMS

Step 1 — Cane or crutch: NOT FEASIBLE. Patient has b/l lower-extremity weakness (hip flexion 2/5, ankle dorsiflexion 1/5) and cannot bear weight independently. Cane/crutch alone cannot enable safe ambulation in the home.

Step 2 — Walker: NOT FEASIBLE. Patient cannot stand independently. Walker requires intact lower-extremity weight-bearing capacity, which the patient does not have. Even with a rollator, balance and lower-extremity weakness preclude safe use within the home.

Step 3 — Manual wheelchair: INADEQUATE. Patient currently uses a manual wheelchair (K0001, dispensed 2018). She demonstrates inability to self-propel sufficient distances within the home for MRADLs due to upper-extremity weakness and fatigue (4-/5 strength, unable to propel >20 feet without rest). She cannot independently complete bathing, toileting, or meal preparation tasks because she cannot get to the bathroom or kitchen reliably under her own power. Manual wheelchair use also creates a high fall risk during attempted transfers and propulsion.

Step 4 — Power-operated vehicle (POV / scooter, K0800-K0812): NOT APPROPRIATE. POVs require the user to ambulate from the device to a chair, toilet, etc. The patient cannot safely transfer between a POV and other surfaces in her home. POVs are also poorly suited to navigating tight indoor spaces such as her bathroom doorway (28 inches).

Step 5 — Power wheelchair Group 2 (K0820-K0843): INADEQUATE for this patient's needs. Group 2 PWCs lack the power-tilt feature that is medically necessary for this patient given her trunk weakness, pressure-ulcer risk (BMI 24, sedentary >18 hr/day), and need for repositioning to manage spasticity.

Step 6 — Power wheelchair Group 3 with single power option (K0856) + power tilt (K0108): MEDICALLY NECESSARY. The patient requires a Group 3 PWC for the following reasons:
  - Permanent neurological diagnosis (MS) consistent with CMS National Coverage Determination criteria for Group 3 PMD.
  - Power tilt feature is medically necessary for pressure relief and trunk repositioning given her seated time and inability to weight-shift independently.
  - Captain's chair seating (vs. rehab seating) is appropriate based on her current postural needs and ATP assessment.
  - Patient demonstrates the cognitive and motor capacity to safely operate the device (MoCA 26/30, intact upper-extremity coordination sufficient for joystick control).

---

ASSESSMENT
1. Multiple sclerosis, secondary progressive, with severe lower-extremity weakness and progressive functional decline (G35).
2. Mobility limitation significantly impairing performance of mobility-related activities of daily living (MRADLs) within the home, NOT correctable by a lower-level mobility assistive device per CMS MAE pathway.
3. Beneficiary is a candidate for a Group 3 power wheelchair with power tilt option per CMS Local Coverage Determination L33789 for power mobility devices.

---

PLAN
1. Order Group 3 power wheelchair: HCPCS K0856 (Group 3 PWC, single power option, captain's chair) + K0108 (power tilt option). Length of need: lifetime.
2. Standard Written Order issued today; 7-element prescription on file.
3. Refer to ATP/SMS at Blue Ridge Mobility Solutions for completion of supplier evaluation including home accessibility assessment.
4. PT functional mobility assessment co-signed for medical record.
5. Patient and daughter counseled on equipment use, expectations, and timeline.
6. Follow-up PM&R visit in 3 months post-delivery for fitting verification.

Face-to-face encounter requirement met per 42 CFR 410.38(c)(2). Documentation date 04/15/2026 falls within the 6-month window prior to written order.

Electronically signed: Robert Klein, MD | Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation | NPI 3456789012
Date/Time: 2026-04-15 15:30 ET

MOUNTAIN VALLEY REHABILITATION SERVICES
PHYSICAL THERAPY FUNCTIONAL MOBILITY ASSESSMENT (FMA)

Patient: Eleanor M. Vance | DOB: 1954-10-05 | MRN: EHR-EV-001
Date of Service: 2026-04-18 | PT: Olivia Carter, DPT, NCS — Neurologic Clinical Specialist
Referring Provider: Robert Klein, MD (PM&R)
Reason for Referral: Functional mobility assessment to support Group 3 power wheelchair PA

---

DIAGNOSIS / IMPAIRMENTS
Multiple sclerosis (secondary progressive). Severe lower-extremity weakness, spasticity, and impaired endurance.

STANDARDIZED OUTCOMES MEASURED
- Berg Balance Scale: 4 / 56 (severe balance impairment; high fall risk; cutoff for high fall risk is <45).
- Timed Up and Go (TUG): UNABLE TO PERFORM (cannot stand independently).
- 6-Minute Walk Test: UNABLE TO PERFORM.
- Manual wheelchair propulsion test (level tile, indoor): 12 feet sustained before fatigue (target for independent home mobility ≥150 feet); RPE 8/10 at termination; HR rose from 78 → 112 within 30 seconds.
- Functional Reach: 4 inches (norms 12-16 inches; severely impaired).
- Modified Functional Independence Measure (FIM) — locomotion: 1 (total dependence).

ROOM-BY-ROOM IN-HOME MRADL SIMULATION (recreated using clinic mock-up + photos provided by patient/family):
- Bedroom-to-bathroom (24 feet, level surface, one 30-inch doorway): patient unable to self-propel manual wheelchair the full distance without assistance. Requires 3-4 rest periods, total time 6+ minutes; not feasible for time-sensitive bathroom needs (incontinence risk).
- Bedroom-to-kitchen (32 feet, level): unable to complete independently. Patient relies on daughter or skips meals.
- Bathroom transfer: unable to safely transfer from manual wheelchair to commode without standby assist; high fall risk noted.

UPPER-EXTREMITY ENDURANCE & STRENGTH
- Shoulder flexion AROM: 0-110 degrees b/l (fatigue-limited).
- Hand-grip dynamometry: R 12 lbs / L 14 lbs (norms for 71F: 38-46 lbs); 4-/5 functional grip.
- Patient cannot sustain repetitive shoulder flexion needed for manual propulsion; demonstrates progressive grip weakening within 15 propulsion strokes.

CONCLUSIONS / RECOMMENDATIONS
1. Patient demonstrates inability to perform MRADLs (bathing, dressing, toileting, meal access) safely and timely with current manual wheelchair due to documented severe lower-extremity weakness, impaired upper-extremity endurance, and severe balance impairment.
2. The MAE pathway has been systematically considered with the patient: cane/walker not feasible (cannot weight-bear); manual wheelchair inadequate as documented above; POV inappropriate (cannot safely transfer); Group 2 PWC inadequate (no power tilt for medically necessary repositioning).
3. Group 3 PWC with power tilt (K0856 + K0108) is the LEAST RESTRICTIVE mobility device that will allow the patient to perform MRADLs in her home safely and independently.
4. PT recommends home assessment by ATP for final supplier-side equipment selection, doorway/turning radius confirmation, and seating system specification.
5. Patient retains cognitive and motor capacity to operate a power wheelchair safely (MoCA 26/30 per PM&R; demonstrated joystick familiarity in clinic).

Functional mobility justification for Group 3 PWC with power tilt: SUPPORTED.

Electronically signed: Olivia Carter, DPT, NCS
License: PT-NC-22841
Date: 2026-04-18 11:42 ET

BLUE RIDGE MOBILITY SOLUTIONS
ASSISTIVE TECHNOLOGY PROFESSIONAL (ATP) EVALUATION & HOME ASSESSMENT
Group 3 Power Mobility Device (K0856 + K0108)

Client: Eleanor M. Vance | DOB: 1954-10-05 | Member ID: UHC9EV00713
Date of Evaluation: 2026-04-22 | ATP/SMS: Marcus Brennan, ATP/SMS (RESNA #ATP-66721)
Referring Provider: Robert Klein, MD — PM&R | Mountain Valley PM&R Associates
Supplier: Blue Ridge Mobility Solutions (Medicare-enrolled, accredited, Tier 1 Heritage MA contracted)

---

SCOPE OF EVALUATION
In-home ATP evaluation conducted at the patient's residence (78 Birchwood Lane, Asheville, NC) in coordination with PM&R F2F (04/15/2026) and PT FMA (04/18/2026). This evaluation supports the Group 3 PWC determination and confirms equipment suitability and home accessibility.

---

CLINICAL OBSERVATIONS (in-home)
- Patient ambulating in current manual wheelchair (Quickie 2HX, dispensed 2018) for the entirety of the assessment.
- Demonstrated joystick familiarity using a loaner Permobil M3 Corpus during in-home trial; able to complete a 4-corner indoor maneuver pattern without contact with walls or furniture.
- Patient instructed in basic operation, emergency stop, and tilt operation; demonstrated independent recall and re-execution after 20 minutes — operational safety adequate.
- Hand control — joystick on right armrest, distance/displacement adjusted for grip 4-/5 capacity; patient successful with light-touch joystick. No alternative drive control needed.
- Recline / tilt motivated by patient's inability to weight-shift independently; ATP confirmed that 30 degrees of rearward tilt achievable with power tilt feature provides the medically necessary pressure relief and reduces ischemic risk.

---

HOME ACCESSIBILITY ASSESSMENT
- Entry: external ramp present (rise:run 1:12), threshold 2.0 inches with bevel — accessible for K0856 base.
- Front door: 36-inch clear width — exceeds the 32-inch turning radius requirement for the Permobil M3 Corpus.
- Hallway: 38 inches clear — adequate for power wheelchair maneuvering.
- Living room and kitchen: open floor plan; 360-degree turning achievable in both rooms.
- Master bedroom: door 32 inches clear — equipment selection (Permobil M3 Corpus, 24-inch base width) confirmed to fit with 4 inches of clearance.
- Bathroom door: 30 inches clear — TIGHT but accessible for selected base. ATP recommends caution; daughter should be present for first week of use.
- Bedside, bathroom, and kitchen turning radii measured and verified suitable.
- One small step (rear yard access) — outside scope, not part of MRADL pathway.

Equipment selected based on home assessment: Permobil M3 Corpus, captain's chair, power tilt, joystick R-armrest, standard 18-inch seat, 70 Ah batteries.

---

FINAL RECOMMENDATIONS
1. Equipment specified above is appropriate for the patient's medical needs and physical home environment.
2. Patient demonstrates safe operational ability with the selected device.
3. ATP supports the K0856 + K0108 prescription as written by PM&R (Dr. Klein).
4. Standard Written Order on file with all 7 required elements; supplier maintains required documentation.

Electronically signed: Marcus Brennan, ATP/SMS — RESNA Certified
Date: 2026-04-22 14:55 ET

ASHEVILLE INTERNAL MEDICINE GROUP
DIAGNOSTIC RECORDS — CONSOLIDATED MEDICAL HISTORY

Patient: Eleanor M. Vance | DOB: 1954-10-05 | MRN: EHR-EV-001
PCP: Janet Holloway, MD | NPI: 1099887766
Date compiled: 2026-04-15
Reason: Pre-PA documentation packet for Group 3 power wheelchair (K0856) — referencing CMS NCD/LCD requirements

---

PRIMARY DIAGNOSIS
Multiple sclerosis, secondary progressive (G35)
- First diagnosed: 2009 (Asheville Neurology Group; Dr. Lee Petersen)
- Initial presentation: relapsing-remitting; converted to secondary progressive ~2018
- Disease-modifying therapy: ocrelizumab (Ocrevus) 600 mg IV q6 months since 2020 — well tolerated
- Most recent MRI brain (2025-09-12): Multiple T2 hyperintensities in periventricular, juxtacortical, and infratentorial white matter consistent with chronic demyelination. New active lesions: none. Brain atrophy: mild-to-moderate, stable from 2024 study.
- Most recent MRI cervical/thoracic spine (2024-11-04): Multiple demyelinating lesions in cord, including one focal lesion at T6 level associated with cord atrophy.
- Baseline EDSS (2026-04-15 PM&R F2F): 7.0 (essentially restricted to wheelchair; able to wheel self and transfer alone)

SUPPORTING DIAGNOSES
- Hypertension (I10) — controlled, amlodipine 5 mg daily
- Osteopenia (M85.80) — vitamin D 25-OH 28 ng/mL (recently corrected from 18); DEXA T-score -2.1 lumbar
- Mild cognitive impairment, multifactorial (G31.84) — MoCA 26/30; not consistent with dementia
- Neurogenic bladder (N31.9) — managed with intermittent self-catheterization (4x/day) plus continence care
- History of falls (W19.XXXA) — 2 falls in past 6 months (urgent care 02/03/2026 wrist contusion; 03/12/2026 no injury)

---

FUNCTIONAL HISTORY
- 2018: Manual wheelchair (K0001) dispensed via Medicare. Patient at the time was able to self-propel for community mobility.
- 2022-2023: Began using rollator for short distances at home; transitioned away as bilateral lower-extremity weakness progressed.
- 2024 onward: Became dependent on manual wheelchair for all mobility; began experiencing increasing fatigue with self-propulsion.
- 2025-2026: Documented progressive upper-extremity weakness (notes on file from 09/2025, 12/2025, 03/2026 PCP visits). Multiple discussions about higher-level mobility device began in late 2025.
- 02/2026: PCP referral to PM&R (Dr. Klein) for power mobility evaluation.
- 04/15/2026: Face-to-face PM&R evaluation completed.

---

LABS (most recent, 2026-04-08)
CBC: WBC 6.4, Hgb 12.6, Plt 244 — within normal limits.
CMP: Cr 0.94, eGFR 64 (CKD stage 2 stable), Na 139, K 4.0, glucose 96 — stable.
Vitamin D 25-OH: 28 ng/mL (improved from 18; supplementation continuing).
TSH: 1.8 (normal).

---

IMMUNIZATIONS / PREVENTIVE
Up to date on flu (10/2025), Covid bivalent (09/2025), pneumococcal (PCV20 in 2024). DEXA monitoring annually.

---

ADVANCE DIRECTIVES
Full code. Daughter Margaret Vance has medical durable POA on file.

---

CERTIFICATION OF MEDICAL NECESSITY (consolidated)
The undersigned attests that:
1. Patient has a permanent and progressive neurologic condition (multiple sclerosis, secondary progressive) that materially limits her mobility within the home.
2. Patient has been examined face-to-face by a Medicare-eligible physician (Dr. Robert Klein, PM&R, NPI 3456789012) on 2026-04-15, within the 6-month window required by CMS for power mobility device prescription.
3. The MAE algorithm has been considered: cane/walker (not feasible — patient cannot stand), manual wheelchair (inadequate per documented PT FMA), POV (inappropriate due to transfer safety), Group 2 PWC (inadequate, no power tilt for pressure relief). Group 3 PWC with power tilt (K0856 + K0108) is the least restrictive device meeting the patient's needs.
4. Patient has the cognitive and motor capacity to safely operate the device.
5. ATP evaluation has been completed; home is accessible.
6. Standard Written Order with all 7 required elements is on file.

This documentation is provided in support of the prior authorization request for K0856 + K0108. Eleanor Vance is a candidate for a Group 3 power mobility device under CMS National Coverage Determination 280.3 and Local Coverage Determination L33789.

Electronically signed: Janet Holloway, MD — Internal Medicine | NPI 1099887766
Date: 2026-04-15 09:42 ET
